all.en: My job as a software engineer keeps me staring at a computer screen for ten hours a day.
all.zh: 我擔任軟體工程師的工作，讓我每天得盯著電腦螢幕長達十個小時。

all.en: My therapist suggested that I try a creative activity to release work-related stress.
all.zh: 我的治療師建議我嘗試一項創造性的活動，來釋放工作上的壓力。

all.en: I signed up for a beginner pottery class at a small studio near my office.
all.zh: 我在辦公室附近的一間小工作室報名了陶藝初學者課程。

all.en: The studio smelled of wet clay and felt peacefully quiet compared to my noisy workplace.
all.zh: 工作室散發著濕泥土的氣味，比起我嘈雜的職場顯得格外寧靜。

all.en: Our instructor was a gentle woman in her sixties with strong, skillful hands.
all.zh: 我們的指導老師是一位六十多歲、有著一雙強壯靈巧雙手的溫和女士。

all.en: She showed us how to center the clay on the spinning wheel before shaping it.
all.zh: 她示範了如何在塑形之前將陶土在轉盤上定心。

all.en: My first attempt collapsed into a wet, shapeless lump within thirty seconds.
all.zh: 我的第一次嘗試在三十秒內就塌成了一團濕漉漉、毫無形狀的泥巴。

all.en: The instructor laughed kindly and explained that even masters started with countless failures.
all.zh: 指導老師親切地笑了笑，並解釋說即使是大師也是從無數次失敗開始的。

all.en: After several lessons, I successfully made a small bowl that actually looked like a bowl.
all.zh: 經過幾堂課之後，我成功做出了一個看起來真的像碗的小碗。

all.en: Working with my hands forces me to slow down and focus only on the present moment.
all.zh: 用雙手操作迫使我放慢腳步，只專注於當下的這一刻。

all.en: I leave each class feeling calmer, lighter and reconnected with my creative side.
all.zh: 每次下課離開時，我都感覺更平靜、更輕盈，也重新連結了自己的創意一面。

all.en: Pottery has become a precious weekly ritual that protects my mental health.
all.zh: 陶藝已成為一項珍貴的每週儀式，守護著我的心理健康。

all.en: After three months in the class, I began designing tea cups with my own personal style.
all.zh: 上了三個月課之後，我開始用自己獨特的風格設計茶杯。

all.en: My favorite piece featured small leaf patterns carved gently into the side of the cup.
all.zh: 我最喜歡的一件作品是在杯壁上輕輕雕刻著小小的葉子圖案。

all.en: The instructor invited me to display my pieces at the studio's small spring showcase.
all.zh: 指導老師邀請我把作品放在工作室小型的春季展示會上展出。

all.en: On opening day, several customers asked if my works were available for purchase.
all.zh: 展示會開幕當天，好幾位顧客詢問我的作品是否有販售。

all.en: I felt nervous about pricing, so the instructor helped me set fair and reasonable amounts.
all.zh: 我對定價感到緊張，所以指導老師幫我訂出了合理公道的金額。

all.en: By the end of the weekend, I had sold five cups and one small flower vase.
all.zh: 週末結束時，我已賣出了五個杯子和一個小花瓶。

all.en: With the earnings, I bought myself a proper apron and a brand-new set of carving tools.
all.zh: 我用這筆收入為自己買了一件正式的圍裙和一套全新的雕刻工具。

all.en: My coworkers noticed I seemed less stressed and more cheerful at the office lately.
all.zh: 我的同事注意到我最近在辦公室看起來壓力較小，也更開朗了。

all.en: Even my therapist mentioned that my anxiety symptoms had clearly improved this quarter.
all.zh: 連我的治療師都提到，這一季我的焦慮症狀已明顯改善。

all.en: Pottery has gently reshaped not only the clay but also my entire approach to life.
all.zh: 陶藝不僅輕柔地重塑了陶土，也重塑了我對生活的整個態度。

all.en: After the spring showcase, my pottery instructor invited me to join the studio's advanced weekend workshop.
all.zh: 春季展示會結束後，陶藝指導老師邀請我加入工作室的進階週末工作坊。

all.en: The new class focused on traditional glazing techniques that required careful timing and steady patience.
all.zh: 新課程著重於需要精準時間掌控和穩定耐心的傳統上釉技法。

all.en: My first glazed bowl came out with unexpected blue patterns that nobody could have predicted.
all.zh: 我第一個上釉的碗呈現了出乎意料的藍色紋路，是任何人都無法預料的。

all.en: The instructor explained that kiln chemistry often produces surprising and uniquely beautiful surface effects.
all.zh: 指導老師解釋，窯內的化學變化常常會產生令人驚喜又獨特美麗的表面效果。

all.en: I began collaborating with another classmate to create a small matching tea set for her wedding.
all.zh: 我開始與另一位同學合作，為她的婚禮製作一套小巧成對的茶具。

all.en: We spent hours discussing colors, shapes and the symbolic meanings hidden inside each design choice.
all.zh: 我們花了好幾個小時討論顏色、形狀，以及每個設計選擇中所蘊含的象徵意義。

all.en: The finished set arrived just one week before her wedding day, polished and carefully packaged.
all.zh: 成品在她婚禮前一週送達，經過拋光並仔細地包裝好。

all.en: Her parents cried happily when they realized that everything had been made completely by our own hands.
all.zh: 她的父母在發現一切都是我們親手製作時，喜極而泣。

all.en: Word spread, and a young couple soon requested a custom set for their wedding next autumn.
all.zh: 消息傳開後，一對年輕情侶很快要求為他們明年秋天的婚禮訂製一套茶具。

all.en: Pottery has slowly grown from a stress reliever into a meaningful side practice in my life.
all.zh: 陶藝慢慢地從一項紓壓活動，成長為我生活中一項有意義的副業實踐。

all.en: The young couple's custom tea set kept me busy through the cooler weeks of late autumn.
all.zh: 那對年輕情侶的訂製茶具，讓我在深秋較涼的幾週裡忙個不停。

all.en: I chose warm earthy tones to reflect the season of their carefully planned outdoor wedding ceremony.
all.zh: 我選擇了溫暖的大地色調，呼應他們精心規劃的戶外婚禮儀式所在的季節。

all.en: My classmate helped me design tiny matching cups for their parents as a thoughtful surprise gift.
all.zh: 我的同學幫我設計了搭配的迷你茶杯，作為一份貼心的驚喜禮物送給他們的父母。

all.en: On delivery day, the bride hugged us tightly and admitted she nearly cried opening the box.
all.zh: 交貨當天，新娘緊緊擁抱我們，並坦承她在打開盒子時差點落淚。

all.en: Photos from their wedding featured our tea set on the family blessing table beautifully arranged.
all.zh: 他們婚禮的照片中，我們的茶具被優雅地擺放在家庭祝福桌上。

all.en: Their wedding photographer asked me for permission to share those images on his professional portfolio.
all.zh: 他們的婚禮攝影師徵求我同意，將那些影像分享到他的專業作品集中。

all.en: Soon, three more couples contacted me through his portfolio website asking about future custom orders.
all.zh: 很快地，又有三對情侶透過他的作品網站聯繫我，詢問未來的訂製訂單。

all.en: To balance work and pottery, I started limiting custom orders to only four couples each year.
all.zh: 為了平衡正職與陶藝，我開始限制每年只接四對情侶的訂製訂單。

all.en: This careful pace allowed me to truly enjoy each piece without losing the joy of creating.
all.zh: 這樣謹慎的步調讓我能真正享受每件作品，而不會失去創作的樂趣。

all.en: Pottery had quietly become my second life, woven gently into the most meaningful moments of others.
all.zh: 陶藝悄悄成了我的第二個人生，輕柔地交織進他人最有意義的時刻之中。

all.en: After two years of careful custom work, my pottery pieces appeared in a small museum's seasonal craft exhibit.
all.zh: 經過兩年細心的訂製創作後，我的陶藝作品出現在一間小型博物館的季節性工藝展中。

all.en: Museum visitors lingered in front of my display, gently touching each piece and asking detailed questions warmly.
all.zh: 博物館的訪客在我的展位前駐足，溫柔地觸摸每件作品並親切地提出細緻的問題。

all.en: A young art teacher invited me to give a guest lecture at her local high school's art program.
all.zh: 一位年輕的美術老師邀請我到她任教的在地高中藝術課程客座授課。

all.en: Standing in front of curious teenagers, I shared honestly how pottery had quietly healed my burnt-out work soul.
all.zh: 站在好奇的青少年面前，我誠實地分享了陶藝如何悄悄治癒了我職業倦怠的心靈。

all.en: Several students approached me after class, asking if I could teach beginner Saturday workshops in their school studio.
all.zh: 課後好幾位學生上前找我，詢問我是否能在他們學校的工作室教導週六的初學者工作坊。

all.en: I gladly agreed and soon discovered that teenagers brought wonderfully fresh, fearless and surprising creativity to clay.
all.zh: 我欣然同意，很快發現青少年為陶土帶來令人驚喜、新鮮、無畏又出人意料的創意。

all.en: One student shaped a bowl in the form of a sleeping cat that made our entire class laugh.
all.zh: 一位學生捏出了一個睡貓造型的碗，讓我們全班都笑了起來。

all.en: At their year-end school exhibition, the students proudly showcased their pottery work next to my own pieces.
all.zh: 在他們學校的年末展覽中，學生們自豪地將自己的陶藝作品與我的作品並列展出。

all.en: My instructor visited the exhibition and quietly whispered that I had grown into a thoughtful teacher myself.
all.zh: 我的指導老師參觀了展覽，並悄悄低語說我已成長為一位用心的老師。

all.en: Pottery had completely closed the circle, transforming a stressed engineer into a calm guide for the next generation.
all.zh: 陶藝完整地畫下了一個圓，將一位高壓的工程師轉變成下一代沉穩的引路人。

all.en: By summer, my own quiet small studio welcomed five returning adult students from busy office careers.
all.zh: 到了夏天，我安靜的小工作室迎來了五位從繁忙辦公室職涯回流的成人學生。

all.en: Each one arrived carrying invisible work tensions tucked carefully inside their tired weekday shoulders.
all.zh: 每個人都帶著疲憊的平日肩膀，無形的工作壓力藏在其中。

all.en: I taught them to breathe slowly while their hands gently shaped soft, cool, forgiving clay.
all.zh: 我教他們慢慢呼吸，同時讓雙手輕柔地塑造柔軟、涼爽又寬容的黏土。

all.en: One quiet accountant cried softly while shaping her first uneven, lopsided yet honest small tea bowl.
all.zh: 一位安靜的會計師輕聲哭了，她正在塑造她第一個歪斜卻誠實的小茶碗。

all.en: She named the bowl after her late grandmother, who had once taught her how to brew oolong.
all.zh: 她以已逝的奶奶之名為那個茶碗命名，奶奶曾經教她如何沖泡烏龍。

all.en: We arranged a small evening exhibition inside my studio, lit only with soft, slow candles.
all.zh: 我們在我的工作室裡舉辦了一場小型傍晚展覽，只點著柔和緩慢的燭光。

all.en: Families wandered between the bowls, listening as each maker quietly explained their personal slow journey.
all.zh: 家人們在碗作之間漫步，聆聽每位創作者靜靜訴說自己緩慢的個人歷程。

all.en: Her grandmother's favorite tea was brewed slowly inside her lopsided bowl, shared with quiet honest tears.
all.zh: 她奶奶最愛的茶，被緩慢地沖泡在那個歪斜的小碗裡，與安靜真誠的眼淚一同分享。

all.en: Several visitors asked to register for the next class before even reaching the studio's small wooden door.
all.zh: 好幾位來訪者甚至還沒走到工作室的小木門前，就主動詢問下一期課程的報名。

all.en: Pottery had quietly grown into many soft tea bowls quietly holding old griefs, gentle hopes and forgiveness.
all.zh: 陶藝，已悄悄成長為許多柔軟的茶碗，靜靜盛裝著舊日哀傷、溫柔希望與寬恕。

all.en: By autumn, the quiet accountant decided to leave her demanding firm and pursue pottery as her main work.
all.zh: 到了秋天，那位安靜的會計師決定離開她要求嚴苛的事務所，把陶藝當作她的主要工作。

all.en: She rented a tiny corner inside my studio and slowly built her own small collection of grief bowls.
all.zh: 她在我的工作室租下一個小角落，慢慢累積屬於她自己的小型哀傷碗作系列。

all.en: Each bowl carried the name of someone she had loved and silently lost during her busy office years.
all.zh: 每個碗都承載著她忙碌辦公室生涯中曾深愛卻默默失去之人的名字。

all.en: A grief counselor heard about her project and asked to refer her own clients to the studio.
all.zh: 一位悲傷諮商師聽說她的計畫，請求把自己的個案轉介到這間工作室。

all.en: We agreed to host monthly grief pottery sessions where mourners could shape clay alongside their unspoken sorrow.
all.zh: 我們同意每月舉辦哀傷陶藝聚會，讓悲傷者能與無法言說的哀痛一同塑造陶土。

all.en: The sessions filled quickly, with waiting lists growing longer than we had ever dared to hope.
all.zh: 聚會迅速額滿，候補名單比我們敢於期望的還要長得多。

all.en: A local hospital chaplain visited one Saturday and quietly observed how clay seemed to absorb deep human pain.
all.zh: 一位在地醫院的院牧某個週六前來探訪，靜靜地觀察陶土似乎吸納了深沉的人類痛苦。

all.en: He proposed a partnership, sending bereaved hospital families to our studio at no cost to them.
all.zh: 他提議合作，將喪親的醫院家屬轉介到我們的工作室，並完全免費。

all.en: Within a year, our humble studio had supported over two hundred grieving people through gentle, patient clay work.
all.zh: 一年之內，我們樸實的工作室透過溫柔耐心的陶土創作，陪伴了超過兩百位哀傷之人。

all.en: Pottery had become far more than therapy for me, blossoming into quiet ministry that healed many silent hearts.
all.zh: 陶藝對我來說，已遠遠超越治療，綻放成一份安靜的服事，療癒了許多沉默的心。

all.en: The hospital partnership inspired three other clinics to refer grieving patients to our small pottery studio each month.
all.zh: 醫院的合作啟發了另外三家診所，每月將哀傷的病患轉介到我們的小型陶藝工作室。

all.en: We expanded our workshop space, building a quiet meditation corner beside the warm humming kiln for tired participants.
all.zh: 我們擴建了工作坊空間，在溫暖嗡鳴的窯爐旁設置了一個安靜的冥想角落，供疲憊的參與者使用。

all.en: A retired counselor joined our team, offering free emotional support sessions after every Saturday clay shaping workshop.
all.zh: 一位退休的諮商師加入我們的團隊，在每場週六黏土塑形工作坊結束後提供免費的情緒支持。

all.en: One widower shaped twelve identical small bowls, one for every year he had been quietly married to his late wife.
all.zh: 一位喪偶的鰥夫塑造了十二個一模一樣的小碗，代表他與亡妻安靜共度的每一年。

all.en: He placed each bowl on his dining table, lighting one candle inside whenever loneliness softly visited his quiet home.
all.zh: 他將每個碗擺在餐桌上，每當孤單溫柔造訪他安靜的家時，就在其中一個碗裡點上蠟燭。

all.en: Our studio hosted an annual memorial exhibition where families displayed pottery beside small photographs of loved ones lost.
all.zh: 我們的工作室每年舉辦紀念展覽，家屬們把陶藝品與已逝親人的小照片並列展出。

all.en: Visitors walked slowly between the tables, leaving tender handwritten notes inside the bowls of strangers they never knew.
all.zh: 訪客在桌間緩步遊覽，在素未謀面的陌生人作品的碗中留下溫柔的手寫紙條。

all.en: By the third exhibition year, over five hundred bowls held quiet notes from visitors across our entire small island.
all.zh: 到了第三屆展覽時，超過五百個碗承載著來自我們整個小島各地訪客的安靜紙條。

all.en: Our small studio had quietly become a sanctuary where grief, clay and warm patient hands gently shaped each other.
all.zh: 我們的小型工作室已悄悄成為一處避風港，悲傷、黏土與溫暖耐心的雙手，在那裡溫柔地彼此塑形。

all.en: Pottery had truly become the soft thread weaving lonely hearts into one steady island of quietly healing creators together.
all.zh: 陶藝已真正成為一條柔軟的線，把孤單的心靈，編織成一座由安靜療癒創作者所組成的穩定島嶼。

all.en: My boss noticed clay stains on my sleeve last Monday and just smiled without asking any questions.
all.zh: 我老闆上週一注意到我袖子上有泥土污漬，只是笑了笑，什麼都沒問。

all.en: I keep a small clay heart in my desk drawer to squeeze whenever a stressful meeting begins.
all.zh: 我在辦公桌抽屜裡放了一顆小陶土愛心，每次開壓力大的會議時就拿出來捏一捏。

all.en: My mug at work is one I made myself, slightly crooked but uniquely mine in every way.
all.zh: 我工作用的馬克杯是自己親手做的，雖然有點歪歪的，但獨一無二。

all.en: Yesterday, my cat knocked over a freshly trimmed bowl, and I just laughed instead of getting upset.
all.zh: 昨天我家貓打翻了一個剛修好的碗，我居然只是大笑，沒有生氣。

all.en: My younger brother visited the studio once and now keeps bugging me to teach him every weekend.
all.zh: 我弟弟來工作室參觀過一次，現在每個週末都吵著要我教他。

all.en: I traded one of my small vases for fresh tomatoes from the elderly farmer at our local market.
all.zh: 我用一個小花瓶，跟在地市集的老農伯換了一籃新鮮番茄。

all.en: Sometimes I sit at the wheel without making anything, just feeling the cool, wet clay between my fingers.
all.zh: 有時候我坐在轉盤前什麼也不做，只是感受指間冰涼濕潤的陶土。

all.en: I bought a small label maker so my students can write their names on the bottom of their pieces.
all.zh: 我買了一台小型標籤機，讓學生們可以在作品底部寫上自己的名字。

all.en: Last week, a tired delivery rider sat down for five minutes and ended up signing up for next semester.
all.zh: 上週，一位疲憊的外送員坐下來休息五分鐘，結果報名了下學期的課程。

all.en: My phone screen time has dropped by nearly half since I started spending Saturdays with the spinning wheel.
all.zh: 自從我週六都跟轉盤為伴，我的手機螢幕使用時間幾乎少了一半。
